raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should the help focus on writing and structure, or on substantive validation of the argument and reported claims?","rationale":{"R1.2":"The section makes a causal argument about contrastive disambiguation driving gains and transferring across settings, so checking internal argument consistency is centrally implicated.","R1.3":"The section links numerical results and ablations to specific claims about gains and bottlenecks, making evidence-claim alignment central.","R1.5":"Because the delegation refers to an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are a central possible responsibility.","R1.6":"The broad request to help with a paper section most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level writing improvement.","RX.1":"Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.","RX.2":"The section contains strong claims about primary drivers, transfer, and retrieval bottlenecks, so overclaim avoidance is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projection rather than actually editing or evaluating the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that justify the assigned responsibilities."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the help focus on writing and structure, or on substantive validation of the argument and reported claims?

rationale.R1.2: The section makes a causal argument about contrastive disambiguation driving gains and transferring across settings, so checking internal argument consistency is centrally implicated.
rationale.R1.3: The section links numerical results and ablations to specific claims about gains and bottlenecks, making evidence-claim alignment central.
rationale.R1.5: Because the delegation refers to an entire section, section-level ordering and narrative arc are a central possible responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: The broad request to help with a paper section most directly entails sentence- and paragraph-level writing improvement.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should disclose uncertainty because the delegation is underspecified.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains strong claims about primary drivers, transfer, and retrieval bottlenecks, so overclaim avoidance is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projection rather than actually editing or evaluating the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid enabling misleading scientific claims or unsupported downstream use.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact features that justify the assigned responsibilities.
